reviews.author: John Doe
reviews.content: The scale of the Great Pyramid is impossible to capture in photos. Walking inside the Grand Gallery was an intense, once-in-a-lifetime experience. Highly recommend getting there at 8 AM.

reviews.author: Amira K.
reviews.content: Magical place, but be prepared for the heat and the persistent souvenir sellers. The view from the panoramic point is the best for photos.

reviews.author: Carlos R.
reviews.content: Standing in front of the Sphinx felt like traveling back in time. The dream stele between its paws is fascinating.

reviews.author: Sarah W.
reviews.content: The GEM is an architectural masterpiece. Even though only the grand staircase is open, the statues of Ramses II are breathtaking. Can't wait for the full opening.

reviews.author: Youssef T.
reviews.content: Saqqara is much quieter than Giza. The Step Pyramid of Djoser is incredible, and the nearby Serapeum is a hidden gem you must visit.

reviews.author: Elena M.
reviews.content: Small open-air museum but the fallen Colossus of Ramses II is massive and beautifully preserved. Worth the quick stop on the way to Saqqara.

reviews.author: Mark P.
reviews.content: The Bent Pyramid is unique. Going inside is a bit of a workout because the tunnels are steep and narrow, but totally worth it for the adventure.

reviews.author: Lisa V.
reviews.content: The Red Pyramid is my favorite. You can actually go inside for free with your ticket and it’s usually not crowded at all.

reviews.author: Ahmed S.
reviews.content: The Valley Temple shows incredible precision in stone cutting. You can really see how the blocks fit together perfectly without mortar.

reviews.author: Marta G.
reviews.content: Old but gold. The Tahrir museum is packed with treasures. The Tutankhamun mask is the highlight, but don't miss the Yuya and Tuya collection.

reviews.author: Hassan B.
reviews.content: NMEC is the most modern museum in Egypt. The Royal Mummies hall is respectful, dark, and very moving. The history of Egyptian crafts is also well-displayed.

reviews.author: David L.
reviews.content: The Citadel is a massive fortress. It’s very well maintained and offers a great escape from the busy streets below. Great history here.

reviews.author: Fatma A.
reviews.content: The Alabaster Mosque is stunning. The chandeliers and the massive dome are incredible. The view of Cairo from the terrace is the best in the city.

reviews.author: Noor E.
reviews.content: Khan el-Khalili is a sensory overload in the best way. Spices, lanterns, and gold everywhere. Make sure to haggle—it's part of the fun!

reviews.author: Kevin J.
reviews.content: Very crowded and a bit overwhelming. It’s beautiful but you have to be ready for many people trying to sell you things.

reviews.author: Mariam R.
reviews.content: Al-Azhar Park is a lifesaver. It’s clean, green, and peaceful. I love coming here for sunset to hear the call to prayer from all the surrounding minarets.

reviews.author: Sam H.
reviews.content: The Cairo Tower gives you a 360 view. On a clear day, you can see the pyramids. The revolving restaurant is a bit pricey but a cool experience.

reviews.author: Chris B.
reviews.content: The Hanging Church is beautiful. The icons and the wooden roof are masterfully crafted. It's built right on top of the old Roman gatehouse.

reviews.author: Rachel F.
reviews.content: Ben Ezra is a small but historically significant synagogue. It’s very peaceful and the architecture is a lovely mix of styles.

reviews.author: George M.
reviews.content: Visiting the crypt where the Holy Family is said to have stayed was a very spiritual experience. The church itself is a Coptic masterpiece.

reviews.author: Omar Z.
reviews.content: Ibn Tulun is the oldest mosque in the city. The spiral minaret is unique and you can climb it for an amazing view of the old quarters.

reviews.author: Layla W.
reviews.content: Sultan Hassan is more like a fortress than a mosque. The scale of the walls is intimidating. The marble work in the courtyard is perfect.

reviews.author: Samer D.
reviews.content: Right next to Sultan Hassan. It houses the tombs of the former royal family and the Shah of Iran. Very ornate and royal feeling.

reviews.author: Alice S.
reviews.content: Walking down Moez Street at night is like being in an open-air museum. The lighting on the Islamic monuments is spectacular.

reviews.author: Tarek M.
reviews.content: Climbing the towers of Bab Zuweila is a must. You get a real bird's eye view of the chaotic and beautiful markets below.

reviews.author: Julie N.
reviews.content: One of the original gates of Cairo. It’s very well preserved and makes for great historical photos of the old city walls.

reviews.author: Ibrahim K.
reviews.content: The Museum of Islamic Art is world-class. The woodwork and the ceramics are organized perfectly by era. Much quieter than Tahrir.

reviews.author: Nadine G.
reviews.content: The Baron Palace looks like a Hindu temple in Egypt. It’s very strange and beautiful. The interior exhibits about Heliopolis are very informative.

reviews.author: Soha A.
reviews.content: Manial Palace is the most beautiful palace in Cairo. The gold leaf, the tile work, and the private museum are just stunning.

reviews.author: Robert B.
reviews.content: Abdeen Palace is very formal. You can't enter the main living areas, but the weapon and gift museums are very impressive for history buffs.

reviews.author: Maya P.
reviews.content: The Nilometer is a cool engineering site. It’s amazing how they could predict the harvest by measuring the water level of the Nile.

reviews.author: Thomas E.
reviews.content: The Coptic Museum has the best collection of Coptic art in the world. The carved wood and textiles are particularly beautiful.

reviews.author: Sophie L.
reviews.content: Gayer-Anderson is a hidden treasure. It’s two old houses joined together, filled with oriental art. It feels like someone still lives there.

reviews.author: Adam F.
reviews.content: The Cave Church is mind-blowing. It's carved directly into the mountain and holds thousands of people. The walk through the garbage city is eye-opening.

reviews.author: Rania H.
reviews.content: The Opera House is the cultural heart of Cairo. I saw a ballet performance here and the acoustics were great. Nice gardens outside too.

reviews.author: Ziad V.
reviews.content: Tahrir Square is a historical landmark, but it’s mostly just a busy traffic circle now. Good for a quick photo while walking to the museum.

reviews.author: Isabelle D.
reviews.content: Zamalek is the best place to live or walk in Cairo. So many nice trees, cafes, and small art galleries. It has a very European feel.

reviews.author: Michael S.
reviews.content: Maadi is very green and quiet. Road 9 is great for food, and the corniche is perfect for a late-night walk.

reviews.author: Hoda M.
reviews.content: Korba has the most beautiful architecture in Heliopolis. The arcades and the old buildings create a great atmosphere for coffee.

reviews.author: Peter G.
reviews.content: The Aquarium Grotto is a bit neglected, but the caves are cool and it’s a very quiet place for a romantic walk in Zamalek.

reviews.author: Monica R.
reviews.content: Saw the Tanoura dance show at Wikalat al-Ghuri. It was high energy and beautiful. The building itself is an old merchant inn.

reviews.author: Abdelrahman Q.
reviews.content: Al-Azhar is the center of Islamic learning. The courtyard is peaceful and it’s a great place to sit and reflect.

reviews.author: Sherif F.
reviews.content: The oldest mosque in Africa. It was recently renovated and looks very bright and clean. Very historic site.

reviews.author: Lucas W.
reviews.content: The City of the Dead is a very unique neighborhood. Seeing the living and dead co-exist is a perspective-shifting experience.

reviews.author: Emily C.
reviews.content: El Fishawy is legendary. You must try the mint tea. It’s loud, crowded, and perfectly Cairo.

reviews.author: James B.
reviews.content: A bit more upscale than Fishawy. The food is decent, but you really come here for the atmosphere and the history of the writer.

reviews.author: Kenza T.
reviews.content: Abou El Sid has the best Egyptian food. Try the molokhia and the stuffed pigeon. The decor feels like an old Cairo house.

reviews.author: Ben K.
reviews.content: The king of Koshary. It’s fast, cheap, and delicious. Don't forget the spicy sauce and the vinegar!

reviews.author: Sara O.
reviews.content: Zooba takes traditional street food and makes it gourmet. The Taameya with eggplant is my favorite. Great branding too.

reviews.author: Dina J.
reviews.content: Felfela in downtown is a classic. It’s a bit touristy but the food is consistently good and the interior is very unique.

reviews.author: Yasmine I.
reviews.content: Sequoia has the best Nile view in Zamalek. It’s great for shisha and appetizers at night. Very trendy crowd.

reviews.author: Hani P.
reviews.content: Le Pacha is a boat with many restaurants. Carlo’s is the best one there. Great for a fancy dinner on the water.

reviews.author: Victoria S.
reviews.content: Birdcage is the best Thai food in Egypt. The service is impeccable and the flavors are very authentic.

reviews.author: Karim N.
reviews.content: Zitouni has a great brunch. The Egyptian breakfast spread is massive and the Nile view is perfect.

reviews.author: Patrick M.
reviews.content: Maison Thomas is a Zamalek legend. The pizza is thin and crispy, and they are open 24 hours.

reviews.author: Jessica R.
reviews.content: Lucille’s has the best burgers in Egypt. It’s a Maadi staple. Their breakfast pancakes are also amazing.

reviews.author: Mounir L.
reviews.content: Sachi is top-tier. The sushi and the steaks are incredible. It’s a great place for a special occasion in Heliopolis.

reviews.author: Oliver H.
reviews.content: Osmanly is a fine-dining Turkish experience. The lamb dishes are so tender and the appetizers are creative.

reviews.author: Salma K.
reviews.content: Bab El Sharq has amazing Lebanese food and a great view of the Egyptian Museum. The grilled meats are perfect.

reviews.author: Tia G.
reviews.content: Pier 88 is the place to be seen. The food is Italian-fusion and the music gets loud at night. Very high energy.

reviews.author: Rami B.
reviews.content: CJC is the home of live music in Cairo. It’s small and smoky but the vibes and the bands are always top-notch.

reviews.author: Zeinab W.
reviews.content: Umm Kalthoum museum is small but beautiful. Seeing her famous dresses and the black-and-white concert footage is very nostalgic.

reviews.author: Andrew F.
reviews.content: The Geographical Society is a hidden gem for people who love maps and old explorers. Very quiet and scholarly.

reviews.author: Heba Y.
reviews.content: Prince Taz Palace is a great cultural center. They often have free concerts or art exhibitions. The Mamluk architecture is stunning.

reviews.author: Farrah S.
reviews.content: Bayt Al-Suhaymi is the best-preserved Ottoman-era house in Cairo. The mashrabiya windows and the courtyard are peaceful.

reviews.author: Omar E.
reviews.content: Family Park is huge. Great for kids with the small train and the open spaces. It’s a bit far in New Cairo though.

reviews.author: Mona T.
reviews.content: CFC is the best mall in Cairo. The dancing fountain is nice and there are so many international brands.

reviews.author: Sandra P.
reviews.content: Mall of Egypt is famous for Ski Egypt. It’s a great place to escape the Cairo heat and go skiing for a few hours.

reviews.author: Mostafa R.
reviews.content: Mall of Arabia is massive. You can find everything here. The food court is huge and has a lot of variety.

reviews.author: Ali Z.
reviews.content: Adrenalin Park is great for paintball. We went with a group of friends and had a blast. Very well organized.

reviews.author: Tamer H.
reviews.content: The Field in Maadi is a nice outdoor community space. Good for working out or just sitting in the sun with a coffee.

reviews.author: Nancy K.
reviews.content: Room Art Space is very cozy. I saw an indie band here and loved the intimate setting. The coffee is also good.

reviews.author: Liam W.
reviews.content: The night light show at the Pyramids is a bit old fashioned but watching the stars over the Great Pyramid is truly magical.

reviews.author: Sophia J.
reviews.content: The Grand Egyptian Museum is going to be the best in the world. The hanging obelisk at the entrance is a marvel of engineering.

reviews.author: Gabriel M.
reviews.content: The mummies hall in NMEC is the most respectful display I've seen. The museum layout is very easy to follow.

reviews.author: Isabella B.
reviews.content: Mosque of Muhammad Ali is stunning but it can get very crowded with tour groups. Go early in the morning for peace.

reviews.author: Noah P.
reviews.content: Found some amazing handmade silver jewelry at Khan el-Khalili. It's a maze, but getting lost is part of the experience.

reviews.author: Mia S.
reviews.content: Al-Azhar park is the lungs of Cairo. The view of the Citadel at night from the restaurant there is unforgettable.

reviews.author: William D.
reviews.content: The wood carvings in the Hanging Church are some of the best Coptic art I've ever seen. Such a peaceful place.

reviews.author: Elijah V.
reviews.content: The climb up the minaret of Ibn Tulun is scary if you're afraid of heights, but the view of the courtyard is perfect.

reviews.author: Charlotte G.
reviews.content: Moez street feels like a movie set. Every building has a story. I spent four hours just walking and looking at the details.

reviews.author: Lucas R.
reviews.content: Museum of Islamic Art has a great cafe too. The collection is so vast you need at least two visits to see it all.

reviews.author: Amelia T.
reviews.content: Baron Palace is a must for photography. The sunset hitting the red stone of the palace is gorgeous.

reviews.author: Mason L.
reviews.content: The gardens at Manial Palace are full of rare plants. The Blue Mosque inside the palace grounds is spectacular.

reviews.author: Harper B.
reviews.content: Cave Church is a testament to the faith of the community there. The carvings in the rock are massive and beautiful.

reviews.author: Evelyn K.
reviews.content: Zamalek has the best breakfast spots. You can find everything from traditional foul to fancy French bakeries.

reviews.author: Daniel C.
reviews.content: Abou Tarek is a well-oiled machine. You sit down, and two minutes later your Koshary is there. Efficient and tasty.

reviews.author: Grace A.
reviews.content: Zooba’s sweet potato with tahini is an interesting twist. I love how they modernize Egyptian classics.

reviews.author: Alexander W.
reviews.content: Le Pacha 1901 is perfect for a sunset drink. The breeze on the Nile makes the Cairo heat much more bearable.

reviews.author: Chloe H.
reviews.content: Sachi has the best service I've experienced in Egypt. The salmon teriyaki was cooked to perfection.

reviews.author: Sebastian M.
reviews.content: Cairo Festival City has a great IKEA. It's my go-to mall for everything I need for my apartment.

reviews.author: Lily E.
reviews.content: Skiing in the middle of the desert is a crazy experience. Mall of Egypt is worth a visit just for that.

reviews.author: Henry N.
reviews.content: Room Art Space in Garden City is very nostalgic. It feels like an old apartment converted into a creative hub.

reviews.author: Ruby D.
reviews.content: Saqqara is an archaeological dream. The tombs of the nobles nearby have paintings that look like they were made yesterday.

reviews.author: Samuel Q.
reviews.content: The Egyptian museum can be hot in the summer because there’s no AC in some halls, but the artifacts are unparalleled.

reviews.author: Victoria R.
reviews.content: The height of the doorway in Sultan Hassan is meant to make you feel small before God. It definitely works.

reviews.author: Joseph S.
reviews.content: Bab Zuweila is where they used to hang the heads of enemies. Grim history but a beautiful gate.

reviews.author: Ellie Z.
reviews.content: Gayer-Anderson is where they filmed James Bond 'The Spy Who Loved Me'. It's full of cinematic charm.

reviews.author: David P.
reviews.content: Al-Azhar mosque is so peaceful during the day. It's a great place to escape the noise of the neighboring bazaar.

reviews.author: Anna L.
reviews.content: El Fishawy has been open for over 200 years. You can feel the history in the cracked mirrors and old furniture.

reviews.author: Robert T.
reviews.content: Abou El Sid's lentil soup is the best I've ever had. It's a warm and cozy place for a winter dinner.

reviews.author: Isabel F.
reviews.content: Pier 88 has great views of the rowing clubs on the Nile. The octopus appetizer is highly recommended.

reviews.author: Julian K.
reviews.content: Umm Kalthoum was the 'Star of the East' and this museum perfectly captures why she was so beloved.

reviews.author: Stacy G.
reviews.content: Bayt Al-Suhaymi is a great example of sustainable architecture. It stays cool even in the summer without electricity.

reviews.author: Mike O.
reviews.content: Adrenalin Park is fun but make sure to book in advance as it gets very busy on Fridays.

reviews.author: Hillary U.
reviews.content: The Cairo Tower is best visited just before sunset so you can see the city in both day and night light.

reviews.author: Kevin L.
reviews.content: The Red Pyramid is massive and the acoustic echo inside the chambers is incredible. A true engineering feat.

reviews.author: Peter X.
reviews.content: The Citadel is home to several museums, including the military museum which is actually quite interesting.

reviews.author: Monica B.
reviews.content: Al-Rifa'i mosque is a masterpiece of the 19th century. The sheer amount of gold and detail is overwhelming.

reviews.author: Arthur M.
reviews.content: Naguib Mahfouz cafe is located in the heart of the bazaar. Perfect for people-watching while you eat.
